title: Sri Lanka treated the $2.5 million Treasury transfer theft as a criminal breach while widening probes into external payment channels.
body_paragraphs: Investigators examined email systems, authentication trails and devices, and the Colombo Crime Division took over the case. The central banker said the institution executed client instructions and did not determine beneficiaries.

A parliamentary committee heard that the fraud occurred across 10 remittances, and that a separate payment stream linked to another country showed irregularities. Analysts said creditors viewed the episode as non‑sovereign, avoiding a default label but demanding tighter controls.
bold_phrases: widening probes into external payment channels
examined email systems, authentication trails and devices

title: The Supreme Court curtailed a proposed tax recovery shortcut and preserved evidentiary rights in the Inland Revenue (Amendment) Bill 2026.
body_paragraphs: A bench ruled that a new certificate‑to‑fine route at Magistrates’ Courts would be unconstitutional unless recovery paused during administrative reviews and Appeals Commission hearings. It also recorded the Government’s withdrawal of a clause that would have permanently excluded late‑produced documents in disputes.

The Court upheld criminalisation of persistent non‑filing after a warning, with fines up to Rs. 400,000, as compatible with due process. Petitioners then withdrew three challenges, narrowing the remaining issues to recovery timing and conclusive‑certificate language.

title: The central bank urged sustained reforms and stronger buffers, warning that shocks were becoming a permanent feature of the landscape.
body_paragraphs: Its economic review said growth at 5% in 2025 followed stabilisation, but the current account could widen in 2026 as imports rose faster than exports. It highlighted the need to keep a market‑determined exchange rate and build reserves to underpin external stability.

Directors cited risks from a prolonged West Asian war to activity and revenue, even as remittances and services supported the external sector. The review targeted revenue above 15% of GDP and a budget deficit below 5% by 2027, following external debt restructuring that lowered interest outflows.
bold_phrases: shocks were becoming a permanent feature

title: The Cabinet moved to tighten environmental regulation with pollutant charges, mandatory location screening and legally binding management plans.
body_paragraphs: Draft amendments introduced load‑based charging so fees reflected volumes discharged rather than flat rates. Developers would have to obtain site‑specific environmental recommendations before operations and file enforceable Environmental Management Plans.

The bill also formalised Strategic Environmental Assessments (SEA, early‑stage planning appraisals) and created licensing for hazardous chemicals, with non‑compliance exposing firms to fines or imprisonment of up to two years. Regulators said the framework targeted wastewater‑intensive and hazardous sectors in manufacturing, energy, construction and chemicals.
bold_phrases: Strategic Environmental Assessments (SEA, early‑stage planning appraisals)

title: Parliament’s accounts committee flagged a Rs. 167 million loss tied to a 2013 loan and unrecovered cooperative debts up to 59 years old.
body_paragraphs: Officials told members that repayments on the building materials loan had stopped in 2015 after the borrower began incurring losses. Auditors also identified Rs. 31 million outstanding from 28 societies, with recovery hampered where entities were registered under provincial statutes.

title: Ministers approved an emergency order for 865,000 vials of meropenem worth $908,250 as hospital demand rose.
body_paragraphs: The supplementary procurement kept the original per‑vial price to avoid delays and cost escalation. The antibiotic, widely used in intensive care for drug‑resistant infections, followed an October 2025 approval for 1.9 million vials.

title: The United States cleared Sri Lankan blue swimming crab shipments after granting a Marine Mammal Protection Act comparability finding.
body_paragraphs: The NOAA certification under the MMPA (a US law requiring foreign fisheries to meet marine mammal protection standards) runs to 31 December 2029. World Bank data showed over $16 million in prepared crab exports in 2023, with more than $12 million sent to the US.

title: A joint storage venture secured investment for 12 grid‑scale battery projects after a revised capital structure.
body_paragraphs: An energy developer agreed to buy 49% of Storex for about Rs. 2 billion, while its partner retained 51%. The portfolio comprised 10MW/40MWh Battery Energy Storage System units (BESS, grid‑connected batteries that balance supply and demand) at a dozen locations awarded by the power utility.

title: An appellate tribunal ordered a retrospective promotion for a former police investigations head to the rank of DIG from August 2020.
body_paragraphs: The Administrative Appeals Tribunal directed the police commission to adjust seniority and service records accordingly. The officer had joined the service in 1986 and served across multiple ranks before the disputed promotion window.

title: The President instructed officials to accelerate land identification and relocation for Cyclone Ditwah‑displaced families in the hills.
body_paragraphs: District data recorded 61,833 affected people from 18,266 families and 93 deaths, with more than 2,900 houses fully or partially destroyed. Agencies were told to prioritise estate workers still in tents by building temporary shelters on the designated plots until permanent housing was completed.
